council_result: S95 Standard batch. Council (Grok-4.20-reasoning ANALYST + Gemini-2.5-pro AUDITOR) CONVERGED: D1 bundle → raise charter to a documented+ENFORCED cap (LOCKED entry + scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs in the build) over a cosmetic 3-KiB trim (ADOPT/ADOPT). D2 SFX → keep new NONET SFX in the LAZY overlay chunk via a tiny audioManager ensureSfxBus() accessor; reuse sfxGain so master/sfx mute apply free (ADOPT/ADOPT). D3 kami sprite → Imagen on solid magenta + Pillow chroma-key (hue/min-channel key robust to the vignette + spill-suppress/erode for fringe), vector fallback retained; Gemini's separate-alpha-mask idea rejected (text-to-image can't produce a pixel-aligned mask). PRIME-AUDIT: bundle check globs the hashed name + measures RAW bytes; SFX null-guard ensureSfxBus; autoplay resume via the existing gesture path. Mid-session: P0 (overlay-never-appears) added — root-caused to a no-catch latching lazy-chunk load.

priorities.label: S95 P0 — CRITICAL: NONET trial fires (music+freeze) but the Sudoku overlay NEVER appears → unsolvable 180s hang (live 1v1 playtest)
priorities.objective: Make the NONET overlay actually appear + be solvable in live play (it never showed in the user's 1v1 — music swapped, duel froze, no board, hung till timeout).
priorities.scope: Root-cause + fix the lazy SudokuOverlay load path; add the missing 2-peer netcode regression coverage; verify the full trigger→board→solve→resolve→resume lifecycle live. OUT: anime SFX/juice (P2), sprite (P1), Voltkin (separate follow-up check).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green + new sudokuSync round-trip tests; live preview solo: overlay instant, solve resolves (solvedBy=local, x2), duel resumes, 0 console errors.
priorities.check_method: S95 P0 (e23b8a8): NONET overlay never appeared. ROOT: ensureNonetOverlay's dynamic import had NO .catch + nonetOverlayLoading latched true forever → any chunk-load hiccup (transient fetch OR stale chunk-hash 404 after the S94 deploy while the tab held the old index.html) = permanent silent ~180s freeze, no board. Overlay code itself is fine (renders+solves+resumes end-to-end in solo — verified in the preview harness). FIX (3 layers): (a) .catch → log + un-latch so the load retries (throttled 1/500ms) + self-heals on network recovery; (b) preload the overlay chunk on the PLAYING-entry edge so the trial pops instantly + load failures surface early; (c) global vite:preloadError → reload-once (in-mem + sessionStorage latch) — canonical stale-deploy fix covering ALL lazy chunks. Added src/net/sudokuSync.test.ts (host→client snapshot round-trip — the previously-untested 2-peer path; 3 tests). Added DEV-only __SPARK__.forceNonet() (tree-shaken). GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1470→1484; live preview verified (trigger→instant board→solve solvedBy=0 x2→resume→0 errors). NOTE: exact production trigger not reproduced deterministically (needs 2 live WebRTC peers) but the silent-hang failure mode is now eliminated regardless of trigger + the 1v1 path is test-covered.

verification.needle: console.error('[nonet] Sudoku overlay failed to load; will retry'

priorities.objective: Ship the illustrated kami sprite so the Codex tile + overlay spirit auto-upgrade off the vector placeholder; resolve the bundle being +3 over the soft 550 charter (raise + mechanically enforce).
priorities.scope: Generate kami → 512x512 transparent webp at public/art/nonet/kami.webp; overlay loads it with vector fallback; raise charter in LOCKED_DECISIONS + add scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs to the build. OUT: parallax sky (future).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green; build OK + bundle check passes; live preview: codex tile + overlay show the kami sprite.
priorities.check_method: S95 P1 (92b7ea8): KAMI SPRITE — Imagen-ultra dusk Ghibli forest-spirit (2 candidates, $0.12), picked the full-body centered one. Imagen gave a rose-pink bg (not the requested #ff00ff), so keyed in Pillow via a HUE/min-channel rule (the character's grey/cream/amber palette never has G as its min channel; the pink bg always does — robust to the vignette; corner diag confirmed bg pinkness 67 vs character -7) + spill-suppression + 1px erode + feather → 512x512 RGBA webp (33 KB, off-bundle), clean alpha on dark AND white (zero fringe). Codex tile (makeTile Assets.load, S94 stub) + overlay buildSpirits (loads sprite, hides vector on success, keeps vector on 404) BOTH auto-upgrade — verified live in the preview (codex NONET tile shows full-colour kami + name + hint; overlay shows the kami right of the board). BUNDLE: raised 550→560 KiB AND enforced — scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs runs last in `npm run build` (parses the hashed entry from dist/index.html, RAW bytes, fails over cap, prints total initial JS); documented in LOCKED_DECISIONS; `npm run size` added. Main entry 553.4 KiB → 6.6 KiB headroom. GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1470/1470; build+guard green; codex+overlay visually confirmed.

priorities.objective: Add kawaii/anime SFX on cell-place/wrong/solve/appear + resolve juice (shake on resolve + a winner-colour screen flood), all in the lazy overlay chunk to keep the main bundle flat.
priorities.scope: New src/render/nonetJuice.ts (procedural Web-Audio SFX + pure flood/colour helpers) imported only by sudokuOverlay; audioManager ensureSfxBus() accessor; screenShake resolve-edge trigger in main.ts; tests for pure helpers. OUT: external SFX assets.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green + nonetJuice pure-helper tests; live preview: SFX fire + flood renders on solve.
priorities.check_method: S95 P2 (d5c54de): SFX layer + resolve juice, ALL in the LAZY sudokuOverlay chunk (Council D2). New render/nonetJuice.ts (procedural Web-Audio) imports only audioManager's new ensureSfxBus() accessor → reuses the shared sfxGainNode so 'M' master mute + SFX mute/volume apply free. SFX: cell-place pip, wrong-grid bonk, 3-sparkle appear shimmer, solve pentatonic arpeggio / lose 'aww' / timeout neutral tone. RESOLVE JUICE: winner-colour full-screen flood (neutral slate on timeout) flashing to the 0.30 photosensitivity-charter cap + ease-out over ~0.75s, drawn topmost+pointer-transparent in the overlay; a bigger one-shot screen-shake on the resolve edge (main.ts) via new pure screenShake.shouldTriggerNonetResolveShake, edge-driven off synced resolvedTick (host+client jolt together). BUNDLE: SFX+flood in the lazy chunk (6.4→8.4 kB); main entry 553.6 KiB (cap 560, 6.4 KiB headroom). GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1484/1484 (+8 nonetJuice flood/colour/arpeggio +6 resolve-shake predicate); live preview via ticker-pump: overlayVisible true, solvedBy=0, flood active in winner colour 0xff3b6b, stage shake offset applied, 0 console errors (renderer paint wedged by reload churn so no final screenshot, but state-inspected + P1 screenshot already confirmed overlay+kami render).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P0 #a-silent-catchless-lazy-import-is-a-time-bomb-for-trigger-critical-ui: the NONET overlay 'never appeared in 1v1' was not a render bug (it renders + solves fine in solo) — it was an unguarded dynamic import() (no .catch) whose loading-latch flag stayed true forever, so one chunk-fetch failure (transient, or a stale chunk-hash 404 right after a deploy) froze the whole duel for the 180s timeout with NO board and NO error surfaced. LESSON: any lazy chunk that gates a critical, time-boxed interaction MUST (a) .catch + un-latch to retry, (b) preload ahead of the trigger, and (c) be covered by a vite:preloadError reload for the stale-deploy case. Verify the LOAD path, not just the happy-path render.

P1 #key-a-stubborn-chroma-bg-by-its-minimum-channel-not-its-brightness: Imagen ignored the #ff00ff chroma request and returned a vignetted rose-pink bg. A brightness/distance key fights the vignette and risks holes; but the CHARACTER's palette (slate/cream/amber) never has GREEN as its minimum channel while the pink bg always does — so keying on 'G is the min channel AND R,G,B make a pink' cleanly separated them regardless of the gradient (corner diag: bg pinkness 67 vs char -7), then spill-suppress + 1px erode killed the fringe. LESSON: when a generated chroma bg is imperfect, key on a HUE/channel-relationship invariant the subject can't satisfy, not on absolute colour distance.

P2 #a-soft-prose-charter-with-no-CI-guard-will-always-drift: the '550 KiB bundle' lived only in handoff prose for ~40 sessions with no LOCKED entry + no mechanical check, so it silently crept over. Raising the number alone would just restart the drift; the fix was raise + ENFORCE — a check script in the build that the deploy CI runs, plus keeping new feature code (NONET SFX/flood) in lazy chunks so the entry chunk barely moved. LESSON: a budget that isn't mechanically checked is a wish; gate it in the build and code-split to defend it.

analyze_summary: S95 ANALYZE: Standard batch, scope-amended mid-session. Headline was a P0 from live playtest (NONET fires but the Sudoku overlay never appears → 180s silent freeze) — correctly promoted ABOVE the approved polish batch under the urgency protocol AND because the overlay is the substrate the polish renders into. Root cause was a load-path defect, NOT a render bug: a catchless, latching lazy dynamic-import. Best win: refused to 'fix by inspection' — empirically proved the overlay renders+solves in solo and the netcode round-trips (new sudokuSync test), which RULED OUT the obvious suspects and pinned the failure to the chunk load; the fix is defensive across all plausible triggers (retry + preload + vite:preloadError reload) even though the exact 2-peer trigger couldn't be reproduced single-browser. P1/P2 hit objectives cleanly; the lazy-chunk SFX/flood architecture kept the main entry flat (553.6/560) and the bundle charter is now mechanically enforced (closing a ~40-session prose-only drift). Honest gaps carried forward: true 2-peer NONET playtest, and Voltkin live-recheck (hypothesized downstream symptom of the broken session, not an independent bug — green unit tests + eager cutscene). Generative spend $0.12. No hazardous findings dropped; preview renderer wedged by reload churn so the final P2 visual was state-inspected (P1 screenshot already confirmed overlay+kami).
